patch.value:
## Context

Search eval harness items pm-22x2 and pm-fhsg are closed. However, a grep for 'ndcg', 'mrr', 'precision', 'recall', or 'ground_truth' in src/ finds no results. The closed items may have been closed as 'deferred/post-v0.1' rather than shipped.

The `src/core/search/relevance.ts` module has the scoring primitives (cosine similarity) but no offline evaluation path.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pm eval` command or `pm reindex --eval` subcommand runs golden-query set against current index
- [ ] Outputs nDCG@10, MRR@10 per query and aggregate
- [ ] Golden queries stored in `.agents/pm/search/eval-queries.json` (gittracked, human-curated)
- [ ] CI gate fails if nDCG drops below baseline by more than epsilon
- [ ] Supports both keyword and hybrid modes

## Evidence

- `src/core/search/relevance.ts` — cosine similarity + rerank helpers but no eval runner
- pm-22x2, pm-fhsg closed — check if actually shipped or deferred
- No `eval` command in `src/cli/commands/`
Search relevance eval harness: implement nDCG/MRR runner and gate in CI

pm-22x2 and pm-fhsg (golden-query eval harness) are closed but the harness was filed as a task/story, not necessarily shipped as a CI gate. There is no actual nDCG/MRR runner in src/core/search/ and no CI job measuring search quality regressions. This means relevance regressions from corpus changes, hybrid weight changes, or provider upgrades go undetected.

- `pm eval` command or `pm reindex --eval` subcommand runs golden-query set against current index
- Outputs nDCG@10, MRR@10 per query and aggregate
- Golden queries stored in `.agents/pm/search/eval-queries.json` (gittracked, human-curated)
- CI gate fails if nDCG drops below baseline by more than epsilon
- Supports both keyword and hybrid modes
